text.title: Rails Decomposition Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Service / Query / Form / Policy / Serializer decomposition for Rails 7+. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every Service Object MUST expose a single `call` (or `.call`) method that returns the canonical entity or a `Result` value object. `void` / `nil` returns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Consistent return shapes let downstream services and jobs compose without refetching the just-modified record.

rule.statement: Query Objects MUST compose model scopes (`User.active.recent`) — they MUST NOT re-implement filters that already exist as `scope` definitions.
rule.rationale: Duplicate filter logic drifts; fixes land in one site and miss the other, opening up bugs that take weeks to surface.

rule.statement: Form Objects MUST include `ActiveModel::Model` (and optionally `ActiveModel::Attributes`). They MUST NOT inherit from `ActiveRecord::Base` or `ActiveType::Object`.
rule.rationale: Inheriting from AR creates ghost columns + persistence confusion; `ActiveModel::Model` is the explicit "form, not DB" boundary.

rule.statement: Serializers MUST list output attributes explicitly. `attributes :all`, `model.as_json` without a `:only`/`:except` whitelist, and `model.attributes` spread are forbidden.

Application code MUST live in one of: `app/services`, `app/queries`, `app/forms`, `app/policies`, `app/serializers`, `app/models`, `app/controllers`, `app/jobs`. Inventing `app/managers/`, `app/operations/`, `app/lib/services/` requires an ADR.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc folders fragment the convention and re-introduce the very thing decomposition fixed.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST resolve records via `policy_scope(Model).find(params[:id])` — never `Model.find` followed by a separate `authorize` call.
rule.rationale: `find` before authorization leaks existence via timing differences for unauthorized IDs; `policy_scope` filters at the relation level.
